heroTitle:
Built by students
who wanted to build.
heroLede: SAST — the Society for Aerospace and Technology — started as a handful of students with one lab bench and a shared obsession with flight. Today it's a full club spanning rocketry, robotics, satellites, and the research that supports them.
storyTitle:
Most clubs teach theory.
We hand you a soldering iron.
storyParagraph1: SAST runs on the belief that the fastest way to learn aerospace engineering is to build something that has to actually work — a rocket that has to fly, a rover that has to navigate, a satellite subsystem that has to survive vibration testing. Members join with zero experience and leave having shipped real hardware.
storyParagraph2: We're entirely student-run, with a faculty advisor supporting from the sidelines. Every launch, workshop, and competition entry is planned and executed by the members themselves.
